channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Tennessee farmers pivot to other crops as looming federal ban on hemp approaches
item.description: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.content:encoded: In some states, farmers must adjust to changes in the Farm Bill that closed a loophole they used to grow hemp for use in THC products.
item.dc:creator: Marianna Bacallao

item.title: Preliminary results of an independent autopsy of Nolan Wells' body are inconclusive
item.description: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells was inconclusive as to the cause of death of the 18-year old who was left on a Mississippi island by friends on July 4th.
item.content:encoded: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells was inconclusive as to the cause of death of the 18-year old who was left on a Mississippi island by friends on July 4th.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: After deadly shootings, police experts question how well ICE officers are vetted
item.description: The ICE officer who shot and killed a man in Maine had a history of domestic abuse and mental health issues. Policing experts say these red flags should have come up in the hiring process.
item.content:encoded: The ICE officer who shot and killed a man in Maine had a history of domestic abuse and mental health issues. Policing experts say these red flags should have come up in the hiring process.
item.dc:creator: Meg Anderson

item.title: People are watching the Reflecting Pool like reality TV. What does that say about us?
item.description: It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.
item.content:encoded:
It's not just watching paint dry. The twists and turns of the Reflecting Pool repairs, originally a two-week project, have kept bloggers and viewers busy all summer.

(Image credit: Jackie Lay)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Independent autopsy 'inconclusive' on cause of death for 18-year-old Nolan Wells in Mississippi
item.description: An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells shows that the cause of death is undetermined pending an investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.
item.content:encoded:
An independent autopsy of the body of Nolan Wells shows that the cause of death is undetermined pending an investigation, according to attorney Ben Crump.

(Image credit: Gerald Herbert)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Her son was killed by ICE at a traffic stop. She says she's still waiting for justice
item.description: Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.
item.content:encoded:
Ruben Ray Martinez, 23, was fatally shot by an ICE agent two months into President Trump's second term. His mother says her family has yet to receive justice.

(Image credit: Brenda Bazán for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: What forecasters expect this hurricane season, as Tropical Storm Bertha edges along
item.description: The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.
item.content:encoded:
The El Niño weather pattern could limit how many Atlantic storms form this year. But unusually warm waters temperatures mean the risk of destructive storms is still high.

(Image credit: NOAA via AP)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: States want to bring Medicaid behind bars. Federal changes make that harder
item.description: Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.
item.content:encoded:
Funding cuts and program changes required by President Trump's big budget law are forcing states to rethink bipartisan efforts to bring Medicaid into jails and prisons.

(Image credit: Ramon Dompor for Tradeoffs)
item.dc:creator: Julie Wernau

item.title: Trump has campaigned more so far in 2026 midterms than he did at this point in 2018
item.description: Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.
item.content:encoded:
Heading to Georgia on Wednesday, President Trump is campaigning more often this year than he did in the same period for 2018 and has already rallied for a dozen candidates in toss-up races.

(Image credit: Roberto Schmidt)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members, Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request, Trump-backed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race in Arizona.
item.content:encoded: Trump will attend the dignified transfer of U.S. service members, Pete Hegseth defends Iran war funding request, Trump-backed candidates won nominations for governor and a key House race in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin
